label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's break this down step by step. The user is asking if following a certain sequence of steps and turns will bring them back to the starting point. The instructions are: 1. Take 7 steps. 2. Turn around. 3. Turn around again. 4. Turn around once more. 5. Take 1 step. 6. Take 6 steps. First, I need to understand the direction changes. Turning around means changing direction 180 degrees each time. So, each "turn around" reverses the direction the person is facing. Let's assume the starting direction is forward, and they begin at the origin. After taking 7 steps forward, they move 7 units in the forward direction. Then they turn around (now facing backward), take no steps yet, just change direction. Then they turn around again (so now facing forward again), and turn once more (now backward again). So after three turns around, they are facing the opposite direction of the starting direction. Then, they take 1 step in the current direction (backward) and then 6 steps in the same direction (backward). That's a total of 7 steps backward from the point after the first 7 steps forward.
